scopeNote: SYBEORG only. No Radi / House of Meroe targets, files, drafts, accounts, or campaigns used.

publicListingSignals: site title references Auto Repair South Omaha
selectedLeads.diagnosis: First-page crawl did not expose a plain tap-to-call link, visible email, or form in HTML; a simpler mobile service-lane page can route high-intent repair visitors faster.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Mobile-first service-lane landing page for repair triage and tap-to-call.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Concrete call-path improvement tied to verified phone/email and auto-repair urgency.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: South Omaha repair service-lane board plus repair-ticket intake; distinct from generic dark hero/form layouts.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: Static local QA passed. Browser visual QA showed a polished, readable desktop page with distinct service-lane/ticket design, clear sample-site guardrails, and no obvious desktop layout break. Mobile CSS includes responsive single-column layout and sticky call bar; mobile screenshot still recommended before sending.

draft.subject: quick service-lane page idea for Atlas Auto Repair
draft.body:
Hi Atlas Auto Repair team — I was looking at your public listing/site and noticed one small conversion thing: in a first-page crawl I could verify the shop phone and Gmail route from the listing, but I did not see a plain tap-to-call path or simple repair request form exposed in the page HTML.

I put together a no-obligation sample concept for a cleaner South Omaha service lane page: call button first, quick repair symptom lanes, and a short pre-call checklist so drivers know what to send before they come in.

Sample concept: https://sybeorg-mockups.vercel.app/atlas-auto-repair/

If useful, I can polish it with your real photos, services, hours, and preferred wording. Worth polishing this up for you?

Reply NO and I will not follow up.

selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Commercial fleet repair is high-ticket enough that one captured lead can justify a focused page.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: Would need fleet-intake and vehicle-issue routing after verification.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup created; insufficient verified proof/assets for a high-quality page today.

draft.body:

draft.subject: storm/roof inspection landing page idea for Recon Roofing
draft.body:
Hi Recon Roofing team — your main site already does a lot right, so this is not a “you need a new website” note.

The specific opportunity I noticed is more campaign-focused: for storm damage or roof inspection traffic, a dedicated landing page could keep the visitor on one action — call/request inspection — instead of sending paid or seasonal traffic into the broader homepage experience.

I can mock up a focused storm-inspection page with your real branding, phone route, and tracking-ready CTA if that would be useful. Worth polishing this up for you?

Reply NO and I will not follow up.

selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup created; generic redesign would be lower-quality than a campaign-specific page.

draft.body:

If useful, I can put together a no-obligation sample page around one service category using your existing brand direction. Worth polishing this up for you?
